details.text: - Literally means: “To stray and get lost (ضَلَّ) from the straight or even (سَوَاءَ) path (السَّبِيلِ)” and it means to be far off the right course of action.
Derivative: مَعْرُوفٍ (Ma’ruf) refers to that which is known and recognised. The Ma’ruf increased as more revelation descended.
Derivative: بُهْتَانٍ refers to a slander, lie or false accusation that is made up and can be spread.

- This can have two meanings: 1) Just like the disbelievers have lost hope from the inhabitants of the graves” i.e., they have lost hope in seeing them or in them being returned to them 2) “Just like the disbelievers from those inhabitants of the graves have lost hope” i.e., they no longer have hope now that their lives have come to an end.
details.arabic_part: كَمَا يَئِسَ الْكُفَّارُ مِنْ أَصْحَابِ الْقُبُورِ

details.text: Derivative: بُرَآءُ is the plural of بَرِيء means to declare your dissociation, disowning and that you have nothing to do with something. To declare to be free or innocent of any association with something or someone.
Derivative: أَنَبْنَا means “we have returned”. It is from أَنابَ – يُنِيبُ – إِنابَة and it means a spiritual returning to Allah; to return to Allah in repentance and seeking forgiveness.
Derivative: أَمْلِكُ means here to possess the authority i.e., to do something or make something happen.

Derivative: الْغَنِيُّ – The One who is ultimately free of need. Linguistically, الْغَنِيُّ is the one who does not need anyone else and completely independent. That state of being is غِناء (Ghinaa’).

Derivative: الْحَمِيدُ – مَحْمُود (Mahmood) is an Ism ul-Maf’ool (اِسْمُ المَفْعُول – That which has something done to it) and means to be praised. حَمِيد (Hameed) is within the meaning of مَحْمُود (Mahmood) however it differs from it because the one who is praising is not mentioned with it i.e., Mahmood requires a third party to undertake the praising. However, Hameed means “to be praised in of itself” in the case where the Hameed has no need for there to be someone who praises him. Hameed is therefore a quality that is specific to Allah whilst Mahmood can be attributed to anyone.
